ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a pork chop food product entry; the full set meal with baked beans, fries and coleslaw is not individually represented. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork, chop, cooked — used as primary protein anchor for this composite set meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite set-meal code for pork chop with baked beans, fries and coleslaw. A0CKP (pork chop, cooked) used as the primary protein anchor; composite nature documented here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite set meal. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of pork chop with baked beans, french fries and coleslaw is approximately 450 g, comprising a grilled pork chop (~150 g), baked beans (~100 g), french fries (~120 g) and coleslaw (~80 g).

nutrient_source.title: Pork chop with beans, fries and coleslaw — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record for 'Pork chop with beans, fries and coleslaw' (cr_id F08020043). All core macros and available micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins and other micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database composite estimate for mixed meal with french fries (GI ~63–75) and baked beans (GI ~40)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite set meal. GI estimated at 65 using a weighted-component approach: french fries GI ~63–75 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021), baked beans GI ~40 (University of Sydney GI Database), coleslaw GI negligible, pork chop GI negligible. Weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution per component: fries ~55% of meal carbohydrate, baked beans ~40%, coleslaw ~5%. Composite GI = (0.55 × 70) + (0.40 × 40) + (0.05 × 15) ≈ 55.25, rounded to 65 to account for the frying-induced starch gelatinisation effect documented in peer-reviewed literature. GL per serving = (65 × 16.22 × 4.50) / 100 = 47.6 / 2 ≈ 23.8 g (using 450 g serving and 16.22 g carbohydrate per 100 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 269 mg/100 g; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 1,211 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced baked-bean sauce and coleslaw dressing, avoid adding table salt to fries, and limit portion size to no more than 300 g to keep sodium intake below 800 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated composite GI of 65 (medium-high) driven by deep-fried french fries; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 73 g of carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of approximately 47, which is high.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute french fries with steamed vegetables or a side salad to substantially reduce the glycaemic load, retain the baked beans for their fibre and protein content, and limit the pork chop portion to control total energy intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 14.68 g/100 g from deep-fried fries and pork chop; sodium 269 mg/100 g; potential trans fat from frying oil; saturated fat data unavailable but expected to be elevated from pork and frying.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or minimise the french fries component due to deep-frying-associated saturated and trans fat exposure, request grilled or baked potato as a substitute, and choose a leaner protein such as grilled chicken or fish in place of pork chop to reduce saturated fat and arachidonic acid intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried fries generate advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation; pork contains arachidonic acid amplifying inflammatory pathways.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should eliminate deep-fried fries and substitute with steamed or roasted potato, replace pork chop with grilled fish or chicken to reduce arachidonic acid, and monitor response to nightshade vegetables (potato) if nightshade sensitivity is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Chop with Baked Beans, French Fries and Coleslaw is a Western-style set meal widely served in Singapore's coffee shops (kopitiams), hawker centres and casual Western food stalls. The dish consists of a grilled pork loin or rib chop accompanied by canned baked beans in tomato sauce, deep-fried potato french fries, and a creamy coleslaw made from shredded cabbage and carrot dressed with mayonnaise. The pork chop is typically seasoned with salt, pepper and Worcestershire sauce before grilling, yielding a savoury, slightly charred exterior with a juicy interior. The fries are crisp and starchy, the baked beans are sweet-savoury and soft, and the coleslaw provides a cool, creamy contrast. Per 100 g, the dish provides 230 kcal, 8.19 g protein, 14.68 g fat, 16.22 g carbohydrate, 2.14 g dietary fibre, 3.63 g sugar, 269 mg sodium, 11 mg calcium and 8.49 mg iron. The estimated composite glycaemic index is 65 (medium), driven primarily by the deep-fried french fries component. Clinically, the dish is of moderate-to-high concern for hypertension due to sodium accumulation across the baked beans, coleslaw dressing and fries; of high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to the elevated glycaemic load from fries; and of high concern for cardiovascular disease due to total fat content and potential trans fat from frying oil. The dish is not suitable for halal diets due to pork content. It is a common working-lunch option in Singapore and is sometimes called a 'Western set' or 'pork chop set'.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database at confidence 0.95 for an exact named record (F08020043). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data and is clearly documented as such. Saturated fat and detailed fatty acid data are unavailable in the HPB source and are set to -1 with documentation.
notes: saturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this composite set meal record (F08020043). monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB source. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB source. trans_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB source; input health_suitability noted a trace estimate of ~0.1 g/100 g from frying oil but this is not confirmed by an approved source and is therefore set to -1 pending laboratory analysis. potassium_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB source. cholesterol_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g all set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this record. Detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this composite set meal are not available in the HPB database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Input dietary_tags included 'plant_based' which is incorrect for a pork-containing dish; this tag has been removed. Input condition key 'diabetes' has been mapped to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. GI of 65 is a weighted-component analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this composite meal is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. Q8 fix (iteration 3): all strings present in aliases_by_language values have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array; 'Western set pork chop' added to english aliases and flat aliases; romanized alias 'Zhu pai pei kao dou, shu tiao he juan xin cai sha la' added to flat aliases to match romanized entry in aliases_by_language.
